requirement: the format specifies its core and how it extends and evolves

findings.observation: The minimal core is named: an entry on factors, requirements, or targets MUST be supplied, with required fields marked throughout the schema.

An Extensions section says applications may add frontmatter properties, report fields, filters, and formats, constrained so they MUST NOT change conforming meaning and SHOULD use non-conflicting names.
Treatment of unrecognized content is defined: tools MUST preserve body content they do not understand and should preserve unrecognized extension fields.

Forward versioning is addressed via the specification-version field referencing the versioning policy; held from outstanding because the versioning mechanics live in an external doc rather than the spec body.

rationale: The spec names the minimal core, how authors add factors/keys/sections, how readers treat unrecognized content, and how the format versions forward. Satisfies the requirement; held below outstanding because versioning detail is delegated to an external policy doc.
